{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26779","id":2367153628,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86NF-Xc","number":26779,"title":"ENH: Add optional `dtype` parameter to `Generator's` distribution methods","user":{"login":"chimaerase","id":13455004,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDU1MDA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13455004?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase","html_url":"https://github.com/chimaerase","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chimaerase/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359239,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjM5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/01%20-%20Enhancement","name":"01 - Enhancement","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-06-21T19:46:38Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T13:41:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

Many `np.random.Generator` [distribution methods](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/generator.html#distributions) generate floating point arrays, but don't accept a `dtype` parameter to control the desired precision.  As a result, the generated arrays have limited utility in some situations, or for downcasting, have to be reconstructed afterward with the desired precision.  For example, `standard_exponential()` has a `dtype` parameter, but `uniform()` doesn't.  Providing intentionally-typed float parameters to `uniform()` still doesn't affect the output `dtype` (always `np.float64`)

```
>>> rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
>>> result = rng.uniform(np.float32(0.0), np.float32(10), 20)
>>> result.dtype
dtype('float64')
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":3,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2473655190","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26779#issuecomment-2473655190","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26779","id":2473655190,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86TcPuW","user":{"login":"patrick-kidger","id":33688385,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjg4Mzg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33688385?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger","html_url":"https://github.com/patrick-kidger","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-13T13:41:06Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T13:41:06Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I think there's value in this. For example I'm using `generator.binomial(1, p, size=very_large).astype(bool)` to draw Bernoulli samples, and this is unnecessarily wasting a lot of memory.

Even if the `dtype=` argument was simply performing an element-by-element cast, fused into the generation itself, then that would provide a lot of value for this use-case.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2473655190/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Reference issue

#### What does this implement/fix?

- [ ] Check TPU support
- [ ] Remove as many skips as possible

#### Additional information

Can do the same for `dask.array` once the problems are fixed over at https://github.com/data-apis/array-api-compat/pull/89.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20085/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":2,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20085/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/comments/2029349921","html_url":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/20085#issuecomment-2029349921","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20085","id":2029349921,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABZIoc549Wwh","user":{"login":"patrick-kidger","id":33688385,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjg4Mzg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33688385?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger","html_url":"https://github.com/patrick-kidger","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-01T08:01:51Z","updated_at":"2024-04-01T08:01:51Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hey folks, I just stumbled across this thread! I see some discussion on the difficulty of using `scipy.something_in_fortran` alongside `jax.jit`.

FWIW I do have a JAX implementation for a fairly large swathe of scipy operations, in the form of [Diffrax](https://github.com/patrick-kidger/diffrax) for differential equation solvers, [Optimistix](https://github.com/patrick-kidger/optimistix) for nonlinear solves, etc. (see the README for a full ecosystem list)

It should be possible to build a scipy-like wrapper for these. (In fact Optimistix already has a simple proof-of-concept `scipy.optimize.minimize` [here](https://docs.kidger.site/optimistix/api/compat/).)

If bit-for-bit compatibility is a goal then these won't be suitable for you. But if these are interesting anyway then LMK, I'd be happy to make any tweaks if they'd be useful to you.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/comments/2029349921/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565/events","html_url":"https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/12565","id":711413710,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU3MTE0MTM3MTA=","number":12565,"title":"__tracebackhide__ Suggestion","user":{"login":"mlucool","id":1813603,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4MTM2MDM=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1813603?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool","html_url":"https://github.com/mlucool","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlucool/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":{"login":"Carreau","id":335567,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNTU2Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/335567?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau","html_url":"https://github.com/Carreau","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"assignees":[{"login":"Carreau","id":335567,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNTU2Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/335567?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau","html_url":"https://github.com/Carreau","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Carreau/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2020-09-29T20:09:23Z","updated_at":"2023-10-30T17:31:35Z","closed_at":"2022-01-11T17:55:45Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi,

As a follow up to: https://github.com/ipython/ipython/pull/12359, I propose we always log the last frame, even if `__tracebackhide__ = True`.

Example:
```
In [1]: def ff(): 
   ...:     gg() 
   ...:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       

In [2]: def gg(): 
   ...:     __tracebackhide__ = True 
   ...:     hh() 
   ...:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       

In [3]: def hh(): 
   ...:     __tracebackhide__ = True 
   ...:     1/0 
   ...:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       

In [4]: ff()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ZeroDivisionError                         Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-4-5cdd2d0a6a88> in <module>
----> 1 ff()

<ipython-input-1-274d332640bf> in ff()
      1 def ff():
----> 2     gg()
      3 

    [... skipping hidden 2 frame]

ZeroDivisionError: division by zero
```

The example above should show hh as the error.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/comments/1785722016","html_url":"https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/12565#issuecomment-1785722016","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/12565","id":1785722016,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAoMVs5qb_Sg","user":{"login":"patrick-kidger","id":33688385,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjg4Mzg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33688385?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger","html_url":"https://github.com/patrick-kidger","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-10-30T17:31:35Z","updated_at":"2023-10-30T17:31:35Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Having stumbled over this a few years layer -- is there any way to disable this, i.e. a `__tracebackhide_even_if_bottom_frame__ = True`? The use case is something like:
```python
def some_libary_decorator(fn):
    def wrapped_fn(*args, **kwargs):
        __tracebackhide_even_if_bottom_frame__ = True
        fn(*args, **kwargs)
        ...  # now do complicated things herre
    return wrapped_fn

@some_libary_decorator
def f(x, y):
    ...  # some friendly user function

f(z=4)  # wrong signature
```
for which I would like to keep things simpler for end users by `__tracebackhide__`'ing the library internals.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/comments/1785722016/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

JaxPrinter","user":{"login":"tttc3","id":97948946,"node_id":"U_kgDOBdaVEg","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/97948946?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3","html_url":"https://github.com/tttc3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tttc3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2022-06-14T09:28:24Z","updated_at":"2022-07-04T12:29:16Z","closed_at":"2022-06-28T11:43:02Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/pulls/23627","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/23627","diff_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/23627.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/23627.patch","merged_at":"2022-06-28T11:43:02Z"},"body":"#### References to other Issues or PRs
Adds JAX ~~and parametric printing support~~ as discussed in #20516 
Parametric support has been moved to #23661.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Allows `lambdify` to output JAX compatible functions. ~~and provides parametric support for all children of `AbstractPythonCodePrinter`.~~

~~The parametric option attempts to provide native support for the problems that @patrick-kidger's `sympytorch` and @milescranmer `sympy2jax` packages solve. I hope this implementation achieves this/will achive this if #20516 is merged?~~

#### Release Notes
<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* printing
  * Added `JaxPrinter`, allowing `sympy.lambdify` to operate on and produce JAX functions. Example: `f = lambdify(variables, expr, 'jax')`.
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

References to other Issues or PRs
Adds JAX ~~and parametric printing support~~ as discussed in #20516 
Parametric support has been moved to #23661.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Allows `lambdify` to output JAX compatible functions. ~~and provides parametric support for all children of `AbstractPythonCodePrinter`.~~

~~The parametric option attempts to provide native support for the problems that @patrick-kidger's `sympytorch` and @milescranmer `sympy2jax` packages solve. I hope this implementation achieves this/will achive this if #20516 is merged?~~

#### Release Notes
<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* printing
  * Added `JaxPrinter`, allowing `sympy.lambdify` to operate on and produce JAX functions. Example: `f = lambdify(variables, expr, 'jax')`.
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

I'm don't think it makes sense to bake into SymPy itself. It make an opionated choice of library to build on: Equinox as opposed to Flax or Haiku. And longer-term I don't think SymPy should need to implement a SymPy-to-Foo for those Foo that have yet to be released. Delegating to to some external library has worked so far [for PyTorch](https://github.com/patrick-kidger/sympytorch).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/1175627107/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592","id":1122982742,"node_id":"I_kwDOBEVPe85C71tW","number":1592,"title":"Ralston's method has the wrong coefficients","user":{"login":"patrick-kidger","id":33688385,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjg4Mzg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33688385?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger","html_url":"https://github.com/patrick-kidger","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/patrick-kidger/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-02-03T11:40:53Z","updated_at":"2022-02-03T11:40:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"So Ralston's method is the 2/3-method, not the 3/4-method:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33688385/152335400-0ee8aff6-fdb2-4069-b10d-72ee6d552845.png)

(Although as you can see it does use 3/4 elsewhere, which is the probable source of the confusion.)

OrdinaryDiffEq.jl currently implements it as the 3/4 method.

https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/blob/d1bacc9dc6d6d7ad070444b65d5415efa761345c/src/perform_step/fixed_timestep_perform_step.jl#L106
https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/blob/d1bacc9dc6d6d7ad070444b65d5415efa761345c/src/perform_step/fixed_timestep_perform_step.jl#L112

---

FWIW I've seen this mistake restated elsewhere on the internet, and in fact it looks like Wikipedia had this wrong for a while. My guess is that someone wrote the wrote thing on Wikipedia at some point, and the rest of the world blindly copied it!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1592/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}
